detail: FULL golden suite (test_golden.py) = 1181 passed / 1088 xfailed / 0 failed in 105s, production mode, at committed HEAD fc7017f900. Gate reported 1-cell regression (1180/1181) but NOT reproduced locally. Large-D suspects (128x512,128x1024, batched n up to 64, KV_DEPTH=1) all pass. Batching is ablation-proven zero-win (compute-bound). Regression is intermittent(race) or environmental. Escalating to static analyzer on batch=true path before deciding fix-vs-park.

description: Structural review of batched SDPA kernels

analysis: Static analyzer: batch=true path structurally correct + byte-identical to per-tile for all supported shapes (full-slot slot-aligned non-straddling reserves; counts balance; no deadlock cycle; get_tile_size==buffer_page_size for all supported dtypes). Regression not a structural batch bug -> rare bursty-NoC transient stall on silicon OR infra flake. Batching is ablation-proven zero-win (compute-bound). Decision: PARK the DM-batching knob at per-tile trivial default (byte-identical to gate-passing R2), keep read_tiles/write_tiles scaffolding as live knob for R3a. Guarantees gate bullet-3 no-regression + bullet-1 no-hang.

detail: After parking DM-batching (batch=false): core unit tests 58 passed (acceptance test_scaled_dot_product_attention + nonaligned + coarse_chunk + debug). Reader/writer now byte-identical to R2 per-tile.

GATE CHECK COMPLETE (parked DM-batching): full golden suite 1181 passed/1088 xfailed/0 failed (== R2 baseline, no regression, ran to completion no hang); core unit 58 passed; precision baseline+matrix+perf/guard 285 passed/112 skipped (flagged-shape soft PCC>=0.997 held, guard set PCC>=0.99). All three gate bullets hold. No SUPPORTED change (perf). Marking R3b [x].

summary: Perf scheme-change: overlap softmax vector phases w/ matmul. Fresh redo after 2 reverts. Key finding: FlashAttention.md §5 lists matmul/softmax cross-unit pipelining as UNIMPLEMENTED future work (FA-3 warp-specialization); on Tensix FPU+SFPU share the single MATH thread + DST, so literal concurrency needs raw-LLK dual-DST-bank hand-scheduling (abandons helper lib -> prior hang/regression). Plan: (1) confirm baseline golden green + measure flagged shape, (2) ablate bottleneck, (3) attempt SAFE helper-expressible lever (tech-report §5 DST-accum / packer_l1_acc to remove phase-10 add) as compile-time knob defaulted OFF byte-identical, measure, keep-if-wins-else-park. Gate-safety: parked knob == byte-identical -> no regression by construction.

note: baseline (reverted R3a HEAD) builds + acceptance green
baseline flagged shape correct in plain mode; --profile run hit a PCC-assert flake but 6 device rows still emitted

summary: Two diagnostic softmax-reduction ablations on flagged shape BOTH REGRESSED vs 5.041ms baseline: (1) exp->copy = 5.335ms (+6%), (2) all reduces cols 8->1 = 8.130ms (+61%). Reducing softmax work does NOT speed the kernel up -> softmax vector work is NOT the dominant reclaimable cost. Contradicts R3c premise (softmax-dominant / FPU-idle-during-softmax). Note: shape-shrink ablations confounded (hit slow helper paths), but robust direction: no cheap softmax-reduction win. Reverted to byte-identical R3a.

lever: fast SFPU exp (Exp<Approx::Fast>) for phase-6 P=exp
evidence: DeviceZoneScopedN (clock-invariant cycles): exp was 54% of per-chunk compute (38548 cyc); fast exp -> 9513 cyc (75% cheaper). Whole-kernel flagged shape controlled A/B same-clock: exact 9.006ms -> approx 5.798ms = 1.55x. Flagged PCC 0.997 soft gate holds. Phase-5 alpha-correction exp kept EXACT to protect online-softmax recurrence.

result: PCC 0.997 held with fast exp; whole-kernel 1.55x (9.006->5.798ms)
